commit.message:
refactor(dev-workflow): Overhaul review protocols with context-aware engine

This commit completely revamps the quality audit and review workflow by introducing an "Enhanced Static Review" framework.

The previous system used static protocols, which were inefficient for targeted reviews. The new framework analyzes the context of code changes to run a more focused, relevant, and intelligent audit.

Key components of this new system include:
- A modular, layered audit architecture defined in '4-quality-audit.md'.
- A Context Analyzer to determine the scope of changes (e.g., UI, auth, DB).
- A dynamic Rule Injection System to apply specific validation rules based on context.
- Refactored review protocols that leverage this new engine for smarter, targeted analysis.

This leads to faster review cycles, more accurate feedback, and a significant reduction in noise from irrelevant checks.
Docs: Enhance and clarify installation instructions for all assistants
Docs: Fix git clone URL in README
Docs: Clarify the Implement & Review step in the main playbook
Chore: Update CHANGELOG for v2.6.0

Docs: Clarify and harden the review workflow protocols

This commit improves the documentation and robustness of the dev-workflow.

Key changes include:
- Clarified the orchestrator/engine relationship between `review.md` and `4-quality-audit.md` in the documentation.
- Added a fallback mechanism to `4-quality-audit.md` to trigger the interactive review menu if no mode is specified.
- Implemented an explicit safeguard in `review.md` to prevent potential infinite loops.
- Added notes on usage shortcuts like `/review` for a better user experience.

Refactor: Generalize and streamline the entire AI Governor workflow

This major refactor overhauls the development protocols and master rules to be completely language- and stack-agnostic, making the framework truly open-source and universally applicable.

The dev-workflow is now reinforced with a comprehensive suite of review protocols for code quality, security, and architecture. This new system is inspired by the successful, battle-tested workflows used by Anthropic's own team to build products like Claude Code.

Key changes include:
- A new, 7-layer quality audit protocol for robust validation.
- Simplified and clarified all workflow steps for better readability.
- Rewrote all documentation to focus on universal software engineering principles.

By adopting these proven strategies for structured AI collaboration, the framework provides a more robust and predictable path from idea to production-ready code.

refactor: Remove DDD architectural patterns and CLI tools

- Move DDD patterns to premium offering
- Remove CLI configuration tool (complexity not justified)
- Rename rule creation guide (0 -> 6 prefix)
- Clean framework to be architecture-agnostic

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Complete dynamic rule discovery implementation

- Update retrospective protocol to use dynamic rule discovery
- Fix task generation to use dynamically located master-rules
- Remove hardcoded .ai-governor paths from README examples
- Ensure all protocol files reference discoverable rule locations

This completes the transition from hardcoded paths to flexible
rule discovery across the entire development workflow.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: Implement dynamic rule directory discovery

- Replace hardcoded rule paths with dynamic discovery using find commands
- Update bootstrap process to locate master-rules and common-rules dynamically
- Enhance task processing to auto-discover rule directories
- Add production readiness validation to task workflow
- Implement mandatory self-evaluation in quality control
- Enhance retrospective with evidence-based analysis
- Add MCP tool awareness to AI collaboration guidelines

This makes the framework more flexible and compatible with different
project structures without requiring specific directory conventions.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Docs: Update CHANGELOG for v2.5.0

Refactor: Enhance AI collaboration and context preservation rules

This commit introduces a significant refactoring of the master rules for AI collaboration.

- The 'Context Window Management Protocol' has been replaced with a stricter 'Context Window Preservation Protocol' to ensure rule accessibility during long sessions.
- A new 'Complex Feature Collaborative Safety Protocol' has been integrated directly into the main AI collaboration guidelines.
- The dedicated rule file `6-master-rule-complex-feature-context-preservation.md` has been deleted, and its core concepts have been merged and expanded into the updated collaboration guidelines for better cohesion.
- Minor formatting fix in `1-master-rule-context-discovery.md`.

refactor(workflow): Enhance dev workflow and standardize rule format

This commit refactors the development workflow and associated documentation to improve quality control and clarity.

Key changes include:
- Renaming and reordering workflow documents for a more logical sequence.
- Introducing a new '4-quality-control-protocol.md' to formalize implementation audits.
- Updating '3-process-tasks.md' to include a mandatory environment validation step and integrate the new quality control protocol.
- Standardizing the rule file extension from '.mdc' to '.md' for better compatibility, as reflected in 'CHANGELOG.md' and file renames.

feat(dev-workflow): Enhance development workflow and governance rules

This commit introduces a major overhaul of the development workflow and AI governance rules to improve clarity, rigor, and automation.

- Adds task complexity assessment and a template for global state management.
- Implements a mandatory rule discovery protocol before task execution.
- Enhances the implementation retrospective with rule compliance verification.
- Standardizes rule creation with strict classification, location, and naming conventions (.mdc extension).
- Strengthens context discovery with dynamic re-evaluation and collaboration checkpoints.
docs: improve context discovery and security documentation rules
docs: improve context discovery rules

Refactor: Generalize context discovery call in retrospective protocol

The reference to the context discovery protocol in  was previously hardcoded to a specific file path and extension ().

This commit updates the reference to simply . This change aligns the instruction with the dynamic nature of the context discovery protocol (), which is designed to locate the relevant rule file regardless of its specific location ( or ) or extension ( or ).

This makes the retrospective protocol more robust and resilient to changes in the project's rule structure.
docs: add local development guide creation rule
docs: add local development guide creation rule

refactor(docs): update references to moved rule file

Updated all references pointing to '0-how-to-create-effective-rules.md' to reflect its new location in '.cursor/rules/master-rules/'.
refactor(rules): move rule creation guide to master-rules
feat: Introduce AI model selection and pre-flight check in dev workflow
docs: update .cursor directory copy command
docs: update dev workflow to 4 steps
feat(rules): Overhaul collaboration guideline & make framework tool-agnostic

feat: add UI governance rule pack

Introduces a new set of common rules for UI development, covering design systems, accessibility, performance, and premium brand components. Updates CHANGELOG.md.
feat(workflow): streamline dev lifecycle and context management

refactor(rules): Enforce stricter context discovery execution

Strengthens the context discovery protocol by adding a non-negotiable directive for immediate execution upon activation.

Introduces a mandatory 'Initial Cognitive Lock' as an internal monologue for the AI to ensure the discovery process is always the first action taken.

This change reinforces the 'BIOS' analogy, ensuring the foundational context is established before any other operation.
docs(rules): Update and clarify rules documentation
repo name update
docs: Refine core principle statement
docs: Final polish on architectural philosophy section
docs: Refine RAG comparison in README
docs: Improve framework documentation and update changelog

docs(readme): Clarify hybrid RAG approach for external docs
>
>Further refine the 'Architectural Choice' section to better explain the framework's pragmatic stance on RAG.
>
>- Clarify that the in-repo system can be combined with a RAG-based MCP server for external knowledge.
>- Add a concrete example, [Context7](https://context7.com), to make the concept more tangible and provide a direct reference.
>- Broaden the use case to include 'external library' alongside 'third-party APIs'.

docs(readme): Refine architectural choice section on In-Repo vs. RAG
>
>Strengthen the project's positioning by elaborating on the architectural choice of managing the knowledge base within the repository versus using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach.
>
>-   Clearly articulate the risks of using RAG for internal codebase governance, such as added complexity, data staleness, and reduced auditability.
>-   Structure the benefits of the in-repo method into distinct advantages: perfect synchronization, unquestionable auditability, and zero-infrastructure portability.
>-   Refine the core philosophy to state that the knowledge base should be treated as an integral part of the codebase for better clarity.

refactor(rules): Enhance Context Discovery Protocol
>
>Rework the master rule for context discovery () to create a more robust and intelligent 'BIOS' for the AI assistant.
>
>Key improvements include:
>-   **Targeted README Discovery:** Implement a hierarchical search for  files, providing layered, domain-specific context.
>-   **Precise Rule Inventory:** Redefine the rule discovery protocol to accurately locate master, common, and project-specific rules based on workspace structure ( and ).
>-   **Enhanced Robustness:** Introduce a 'kernel panic' failsafe if the critical collaboration rule is missing and clarify caching mechanisms to prevent redundant file reads.
>-   **Dynamic Re-evaluation:** Add a protocol for the AI to dynamically re-evaluate its context when a significant shift in the task's scope is detected.

docs: Refine workflow from 5 to 4 steps

Removes the "Improve/Retrospective" step from the development lifecycle across the documentation. This streamlines the core workflow to 4 steps (Bootstrap, Define, Plan, Implement), making the process more concise.

docs: Refine and merge community call-to-action

Simplifies and consolidates the community and getting started calls-to-action into a single, more prominent and effective block in the main README. This improves clarity and provides a more streamlined user journey from installation to community engagement.

docs: Enhance clarity and user guidance across documentation

This commit improves the documentation to provide clearer guidance for users of different AI assistants.

- Clarifies in  that the  directory structure is also supported for Claude and OpenCode via their boot sequence files.
- Corrects duplicated paths in  and  boot sequences.
- Adds direct links in the workflow guide to the specific boot sequences for Claude and OpenCode users.
- Makes the call-to-action link to the main workflow guide more prominent in the root .

docs(workflow): Overhaul and simplify workflow README

This commit introduces a major refactoring of the dev-workflow README to significantly improve clarity, simplicity, and user experience.

Key changes include:
- Merged the descriptive "How it Works" section with the "Practical Guide" into a single, unified, and more intuitive workflow guide.
- Drastically simplified the installation process by replacing the multi-step, temporary folder method with a direct  into an  directory.
- Unified the setup instructions, providing a clear and concise path for both Cursor and other AI assistant users.

docs(workflow): Refine and clarify dev-workflow README

Renumbers the main workflow steps to start from 0 for the initial bootstrap, improving the logical flow.

Unifies and simplifies the installation guide into a single, clearer process for all users, removing the distinction between new and existing projects.

Adds specific, contextual recommendations for using Cursor's "Max Mode" on steps where it provides the most benefit (0, 1, and 2).

docs(readme): Refine and clarify project documentation

This commit includes a series of improvements to the README files based on an iterative review process.

- Restructured the main README to clarify the dual-component architecture.
- Moved the detailed installation guide to the dev-workflow README.
- Corrected the installation procedure for existing projects to be safer.
- Removed all HTML tags to ensure pure Markdown.
- Refined wording for a more impactful and inclusive message.

feat(branding): Overhaul project identity to 'The Governor Framework'

This major strategic update rebrands the project to better align with its core mission: providing robust governance for AI-driven software development.

The new identity, 'The Governor Framework - The Keystone for AI-Driven Code', moves beyond simple AI assistance to focus on active governance, addressing the growing developer need for control, consistency, and quality.

This commit includes:
- Complete rebranding to 'The Governor Framework'.
- Rewriting all READMEs for clarity, impact, and a new 'Why, How, What' structure.
- Adopting the 'Template Repository' model to simplify project adoption.
- Simplifying the bootstrap protocol to align with the new template-based workflow.

fix: restore front-matter to context discovery rule

This commit restores the YAML front-matter to the '1-master-rule-context-discovery.md' file. This metadata, including the 'alwaysApply: true' flag, is critical for the AI's boot sequence to correctly identify and load this rule as a foundational part of its operational context.

feat: introduce AI boot sequence and refine context discovery
This commit introduces a structured boot sequence for AI assistants by adding  and . These files define a clear initialization protocol that directs the AI to load kernel rules from the  directory before proceeding with any task.

Key changes include:
- Added  to exclude common unnecessary files.
- Created  and  to establish a consistent boot process for different AI agents.
- Updated  to set  to .
- Refactored  to clarify the AI's role as a System Architect, define strict and guideline prefixes, and optimize context loading by avoiding redundant file reads.

feat(rules): Fortify context-discovery BIOS with stricter protocols

- Refine the '1-master-rule-context-discovery.md' to act as a robust system BIOS.
- Introduce a 'System Architect' persona to guide the context-building process.
- Enforce a strict, non-negotiable boot sequence for rule evaluation and loading.
- Mandate a precise and user-friendly communication format for announcing loaded rules, including explicit examples and anti-patterns.

refactor(rules): Implement efficient, BIOS-like context discovery

- Establish '1-master-rule-context-discovery.mdc' as the sole 'alwaysApply: true' rule, acting as a system BIOS to orchestrate the loading of other rules. This hierarchical approach minimizes initial token cost.
- Strengthen rule creation guidelines in '0-how-to-create-effective-rules.md' with '[STRICT]' and '[GUIDELINE]' prefixes for unambiguous directives.
- Mandate explicit positive (✅) and negative (❌) examples for all rules to improve clarity.
- Streamline 'README.md' to clarify the new boot sequence and the role of master rules as the AI's 'kernel'.
docs(rules): Clarify that alwaysApply must be false for project-rules
fix(docs): Correct unclosed markdown block in rule example

feat(rules): Enhance rule system for Cursor compatibility

This commit refines the rule definition and the development workflow to ensure robust compatibility with Cursor's .mdc rule system.

Key changes include:
- A new conditional step in 0-bootstrap-your-project.md to configure master rules for Cursor, including file renaming to .mdc and adding YAML metadata.
- A stricter YAML frontmatter schema in 0-how-to-create-effective-rules.md, now allowing only description and alwaysApply keys.
- Detailed implementation notes for creating and modifying .mdc files to prevent metadata corruption.
docs: Add execution instructions for bootstrap protocol
docs: Add missing reference to workflow README
refactor(workflow): Transform bootstrap into autonomous context engineering protocol
docs: Finalize workflow presentation in READMEs

docs: Refactor and clarify core documentation

- Restructure main README and dev-workflow README for better clarity and separation of concerns.
- Introduce a 'Why' for each workflow phase to explain the underlying rationale.
- Refine the 'Goal' and 'Role' for each phase to be more specific and action-oriented.
- Consolidate the workflow instructions in 'dev-workflow/README.md' into a clear, sequential list.

docs: Streamline and clarify project workflow

This commit significantly refactors the main README.md to improve clarity and provide a more intuitive user experience.

- Merges the 'Quickstart' and 'Workflow at a Glance' sections into a single, cohesive overview, eliminating redundancy.
- Adds a direct link to the dev-workflow README for detailed step-by-step instructions.
- The resulting documentation is more concise and effectively guides new users to the automated bootstrap process.

refactor(docs): Rendre le framework entièrement 'plug & play' et piloté par l'IA

Révision complète de la documentation et du workflow pour une approche 'plug & play'. Le framework est désormais entièrement bootstrappé par une seule commande de l'IA, éliminant toute configuration manuelle pour l'utilisateur.

- Le protocole '0-bootstrap-your-project.md' est maintenant un script d'installation automatisé qui crée les répertoires et copie les règles.
- Le README principal et celui du dev-workflow orientent directement les utilisateurs vers ce script de bootstrap.
- Le README des règles a été simplifié pour ne conserver que les informations conceptuelles, supprimant les instructions d'installation désormais obsolètes.
- La documentation a été rendue agnostique à l'outil, en distinguant clairement les instructions obligatoires pour Cursor des recommandations générales.
docs: Update README.md

Refactor(retrospective): Replace static checklists with dynamic, rule-based audit

The  protocol has been significantly enhanced:

- **Removed Static Checklists:** The rigid, example-based checklists for frontend and backend have been removed.
- **Introduced Dynamic Audit:** The protocol now mandates invoking  to load project-specific rules.
- **Rule-Based Compliance:** The AI must now audit the code by systematically checking for compliance against the dynamically loaded rules.
- **Scoped Review:** The new process requires the AI to identify the code's scope (e.g., UI, service) and filter relevant rules, making the review more targeted and intelligent.

This change transforms the retrospective from a generic review into a precise, context-aware compliance audit.

Docs: Refine framework philosophy to 'Context is King'

The README has been updated to replace the 'From Prompting to Governing' section with a more powerful 'Context is King' philosophy.

This change introduces four core principles of Context Engineering:
1. Decompose Complexity
2. Enable Targeted Access
3. Keep Humans in the Loop
4. Evolve Context Continuously

This provides a clearer, more strategic vision for how the framework enables effective AI collaboration.
Style: Fix trailing whitespace in README
Docs: Clarify framework's suitability for complex monorepos

Docs: Clarify and enhance project-specific rules section

The  has been updated to:
- Introduce a dedicated  category as the most important type of rule.
- Provide a clearer, step-by-step guide on how to implement master, common, and project-specific rules.
- Emphasize the value of creating custom rules within a specific codebase to define its unique standards.

Refactor: Overhaul framework for bootstrap autonomy and clarity
This commit introduces a major refactoring of the AI Dev Assistant Framework to improve its usability and onboarding process.

Key changes include:
- A new  protocol to analyze a codebase and create a foundational 'Context Kit.'
- The 'Companion Expert' concept is now central, clarifying the AI's evolving role.
- Documentation is updated to guide users through a more intuitive workflow, starting with the bootstrap protocol.
- The framework's language is generalized to be less Cursor-specific and more adaptable to various AI assistants.

Initial commit: Add the complete AI Dev Assistant Framework

readme_html:
AI Governor Framework

The Keystone for AI-Driven Code

Stop fighting your AI assistant. Start governing it.

Tired of AI-generated code that's buggy, inconsistent, and ignores your architecture? The AI Governor Framework is a safe, plug-and-play system designed to teach your AI your project's unique DNA. It provides a set of rules and workflows to turn any AI assistant from a chaotic tool into a disciplined engineering partner that respects your architectural decisions, best practices, and non-negotiable constraints.

Reclaim control. Enforce your coding standards. Ship with confidence.

⚠️ This project has been superseded by GAAI — Governed Agentic AI Infrastructure.

GAAI is the production-ready evolution of this framework — dual-track agents, persistent memory, skill-based execution, and a full decision trail. Battle-tested on 176 stories, 177 decisions, 84,000 lines of TypeScript.

→ Go to GAAI

✨ The Philosophy: From Prompting & Fixing to Governing

This approach is rooted in one core principle: Context Engineering.

This isn't about bigger prompts or dumping your entire codebase into one, which is both ineffective and expensive. It's about giving the AI the right information at the right time. This framework achieves that by building a knowledge base of robust rules (the orders) and architectural READMEs (the context) that the AI consults on-demand.

Architectural Choice: An In-Repo Knowledge Base

This framework is built on a simple, robust principle: Treat your project's knowledge base like your codebase.

We leverage an In-Repo approach, keeping all governance rules and architectural context directly inside the repository. This makes the AI's knowledge base:

  • Simple & Efficient: Zero network latency and no complex external systems.
  • Evolutive & Maintainable: The AI's context evolves in lock-step with your code.
  • Auditable & Versioned: Every change is tracked in git, providing a clear, human-readable history.
  • Portable & Robust: Any developer can clone the repo and have the full, up-to-date context instantly, ensuring stability and consistency.

For complex external documentation, such as third-party APIs or external library, this in-repo system can be seamlessly combined with a RAG-based MCP server, such as Context7, to fetch and inject that external knowledge on demand. This leverages the best of both worlds: robust and versioned in-Repo governance for your internal architecture, and dynamic, on-demand context for external dependencies.

This is how we shift from the endless loop of prompting and fixing to strategic Governing.

🚀 How It Works: Two Core Components

The AI Governor Framework is composed of two distinct but complementary parts:

Component What It Is How It's Used
The Governance Engine (/rules) A set of powerful, passive rules that run in the background. Your AI assistant discovers and applies these rules automatically to ensure quality and consistency.
The Operator's Playbook (/dev-workflow) A set of active, step-by-step protocols for the entire development lifecycle. You manually invoke these protocols to guide the AI through complex tasks like planning and implementation.

A Battle-Tested Workflow for Quality at Scale

The dev-workflow is now reinforced with a comprehensive suite of review protocols for code quality, security, and architecture. This new system is inspired by the successful, battle-tested workflows used by Anthropic's own team to build products like Claude Code.

Key changes include:

  • A new, 7-layer quality audit protocol for robust validation.
  • Simplified and clarified all workflow steps for better readability.
  • Rewrote all documentation to focus on universal software engineering principles.

By adopting these proven strategies for structured AI collaboration, the framework provides a more robust and predictable path from idea to production-ready code.

At a Glance: The Operator's Playbook

The framework is built around a series of sequential protocols that move a feature from idea to production with clarity and control:

  • 0️⃣ Bootstrap: Turns a generic AI into a project-specific expert. (One-Time Setup)
  • 1️⃣ Define: Transforms an idea into a detailed PRD.
  • 2️⃣ Plan: Converts the PRD into a step-by-step technical plan.
  • 3️⃣ Implement & Review: Executes the plan, followed by a mandatory quality audit (4-quality-audit.md) and a process retrospective (5-implementation-retrospective.md) to continuously improve the system.

▶️ Get Started

Ready to install the framework and run your first governed task?

3. Quick Start: Installation

This guide provides a safe, non-destructive process to integrate the framework into any project.

1. Clone the Framework

Open a terminal at the root of your project and run the following command:

git clone https://github.com/Fr-e-d/AI-Governor-Framework.git .ai-governor

This downloads the entire framework into a hidden .ai-governor directory within your project.

2. Configure for Your Assistant

The final step depends on your AI assistant. Choose the relevant section below.

For Cursor Users

To get the best experience, including custom commands like /review, copy both the rules and the prompts into your project's .cursor directory.

# Copy governance rules
mkdir -p .cursor/rules && cp -r .ai-governor/rules/* .cursor/rules/

# Copy custom prompts for an enhanced workflow
mkdir -p .cursor/prompts && cp -r .ai-governor/.cursor/prompts/* .cursor/prompts/

For Claude Code Users

To enable custom commands like /review, copy the command definitions into your project's .claude directory.

mkdir -p .claude/commands && cp -r .ai-governor/.claude/commands/* .claude/commands/

For Other AI Assistants (e.g., OpenCode)

The framework is ready to use without extra steps. Your assistant will find its boot sequence (CLAUDE.md or OpenCode.md) and the necessary rules within the .ai-governor directory.

[!NOTE]

Ready to Start?

➡️ Go to the Full Workflow Guide

Got questions or ideas?

🗣️ Join the Community on GitHub Discussions

❤️ Support This Project

If you find this framework valuable, please consider showing your support. It is greatly appreciated!

  • Sponsor on GitHub

🤝 Attribution & License

This framework is an enhanced and structured adaptation inspired by the foundational work on AI-driven development by snarktank/ai-dev-tasks.

It is shared under the Apache 2.0 License. See the LICENSE file for more details. For contribution guidelines, please see CONTRIBUTING.md.
